app.title: Theme Editor
composite.name: Theme name
selector.label: Theme to edit
iconPack.noneInstalled: No icon packs installed yet — install one to override the built-in glyphs.
typo.name: Typography name
stylePack.name: Style pack name
stylePack.hint: Raw CSS layered over the theme. Target stable chrome anchors like [data-bs-region="dashboard-header"]. No @import, scripts, or external URLs.
stylePack.openHintSaveFirst: Save the theme first to edit the CSS in the Code Editor.
deps.message: This theme references components that aren't installed. Reset each to its default to make the theme resolvable.
action.previewShellHint: Paint this theme across the dashboard + open apps for a few seconds.
status.seeded: New theme seeded from the default.
status.forked: Editing a copy — Save to keep your changes.
status.offline: Running outside the shell — changes are in-memory only.
status.openSaveFirst: Save the theme first to edit its CSS in the Code Editor.
status.openedInCodeEditor: Opened the style pack's CSS in the Code Editor.
status.openFailed: Could not open the Code Editor.
status.previewing: Previewing across the shell — it'll revert shortly.
status.previewOffline: Cross-shell preview needs the running shell.
preview.label: Preview
preview.caption: A glimpse of your theme across the app.
preview.notes.title: Welcome to your theme
preview.notes.body: Edit a colour, font, or icon pack on the left — this preview re-paints instantly so you can see the result in context.
preview.tasks.title: Sprint planning
preview.tasks.body: Three tasks are due today. Accent, surface, and border tokens show through the checkboxes, chips, and the primary action.
preview.files.title: Project assets
preview.files.body: Folders and files inherit your surface and text tokens, so the file tree stays legible under whatever theme you build.
preview.calendar.title: This week
preview.calendar.body: Event chips and the today marker ride the accent token — a quick way to check contrast against your background.
preview.settings.title: Appearance
preview.settings.body: Controls, toggles, and section headers exercise the full token set so settings stays readable in every theme.
